IT APPEARS FROM THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS THAT AN AMENDED PETITION WAS FILED AFTER RESPONDENT'S OPPOSITION AND THEN APPEARS TO HAVE BEEN SUPPLEMENTED 5 DAYS LATER BY A PURPORTED EXHIBIT TO THE PETITION. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONER STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN, JULIA CHOPE MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THIS PETITION IS THE PROPER MECHANISM FOR AN "ACTION IN CIVIL COURT TO COLLECT THE AMOUNT OWED" REFERENCED IN THE RENT BOARD DECISION. MOVING PARTY ALSO FAILS TO PROVIDE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE STANDARD OF REVIEW OR ANY REQUIREMENTS FOR THE MOVING PARTY TO PREVAIL IN THE "CIVIL ACTION" CONTEMPLATED BY THE RENT BOARD DECISION. THE FOLLOWING ARGUMENT IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IS NOT FOLLOWED BY A CITATION TO A STATUTE, ORDINANCE OR CASE LAW: ""TO PREVAIL, TENANTS NEED ONLY PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT 1) THE RENT BOARD ISSUED A DECISION ORDERING LANDLORD TO MAKE PAYMENT TO TENANTS IN THE AMOUNT NOW REQUESTED; 2) THE RENT BOARD'S DECISION IS FINAL; AND 3) LANDLORD HAS NOT MADE PAYMENT TO TENANTS." (MOVING POINTS AND AUTHORITIES 5:17-20). UNAUTHORIZED SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION AND REPLY FILED MONTHS AFTER THE ORIGINAL HEARING DATE WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT ARE STRICKEN. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; REPORTED. JUDITH DEALBA, CSR 5709, 650-571-1473, JUDY@DEALBAREPORTINGSERVICE.COM (D501)

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Nancy Conway, Law Offices of Nancy Conway, 415-241-1140, via zoom for and with petitioner Stephan Howsepian
Curtis Dowling, Dowling & Marques, LLP 415-977-0444, via zoom for respondent

Ms. Conway contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN, JULIA CHOPE MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that this petition is the proper mechanism for an "action in civil court to collect the amount owed" referenced in the Rent Board decision. Moving party also fails to provide any authority for the standard of review or any requirements for the moving party to prevail in the "civil action" contemplated by the Rent Board decision. The following argument in the Points and Authorities is not followed by a citation to a statute, ordinance or case law: ""To prevail, Tenants need only provide sufficient evidence to show that 1) the Rent Board issued a Decision ordering Landlord to make payment to Tenants in the amount now requested; 2) the Rent Board's decision is final; and 3) Landlord has not made payment to Tenants." (Moving Points and Authorities 5:17-20). Unauthorized supplemental opposition and reply filed months after the original hearing date without leave of Court are stricken.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reported. Judith Dealba, CSR 5709, 650-571-1473, judy@dealbareportingservice.com (D501)

Additionally, no later than January 31, 2025 opposing party to file a supplemental brief, not to exceed 2 pages, addressing authority allowing for filing of "supplement to respondents opposition" months after the original hearing date and after the opposition having been filed. Order continuing the hearing date did not authorize any additional filings.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
